name: Christos Giannakopoulos
label: Physics Ph.D.
summary:
Research Scientist with a Physics Ph.D. and programming experience in Python, SQL, MATLAB, and HTML/CSS  and strong fundamental knowledge of Machine Learning, all forged in the crucible of experimental Cosmology. Passionate about working with data and developing predictive models to aid data-driven decisions leveraging cutting-edge analysis tools. 
 
 For a formal introduction, please refer to my resume. For more details about my various sideprojects, please click the 'See Projects' button below.

work_experience.summary: Ph.D. student researcher part of the BICEP/Keck Collaboration: a federally funded experimental cosmology program leading the field in measurements of the Cosmic Microwave Background (CMB) polarization.
highlights: Built an end-to-end NLP pipeline using spaCy to mine large, multi-source unstructured datasets which enabled named entity recognition and relationship extraction to reduce manual review time of scientific literature.

Conducted extensive exploratory data analysis on high-dimensional, and multi-modal sensor data. Engineered new features through domain-informed PCA/SVD and dimensionality reduction to feed scalable end-to-end pipelines.

Built and optimized predictive models on complex, unstructured datasets (time series, point clouds, spatial maps) using Bayesian inference and Gaussian processes, improving detection sensitivity by 2×.
Maintained large-scale data pipelines processing multi-terabyte datasets on distributed computing clusters, consistently delivering production-quality outputs under tight deadlines.

Designed statistical validation tools that cross-validated model outputs against independent measurements and met analysis deadlines 50% sooner while enabling data-driven decisions for program leadership.
Developed model validation frameworks that quantified previously unmeasured system uncertainties, informing hardware design trade-offs and achieving a 78% reduction in compute costs.
Led a cross-functional team on a six-month project whose published results became the collaboration-wide analysis standard. (see publications below)
Designed scalable algorithms that improved pipeline runtime by 8×, enabling near real-time data quality monitoring across a distributed high-performance computing infrastructure.
Architected a self-correcting RAG custom agent leveraging the 8B Llama 3.1 LLM to provide domain-specific Q&A.
Mentored junior researchers in statistical methods, analysis workflows, and best practices while serving as subject matter expert for data system performance and model validation.
Became an expert in: PCA/SVD, Bayesian statistics/inference, Multi-component maximum likelihood analysis
Developed expertise in: Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning, Transfer Learning, Anomaly Detection, Clustering, Decision Trees, Random Forests, Convolutional Neural Networks
Simulated real time-ordered CMB data and analysis using advanced mathematical methods, (spherical convolutions) without manifold connection information.
Taught and coordinated physics labs and recitations for 200+ students.
Utilized active learning techniques to stimulate student engagement to maximize learning outcomes.
Bolstered learning of complex physics concepts through problem-solving sessions.
Taught and tutored undergraduates on the fundamentals of Physics
Characterized Pulsar Wind Nebulae phenomenology using data from the Sardinia Radio Telescope (SRT)

projects.preamble: This section lists a few projects I work on while pursuing my Physics Ph.D.; they reflect my interests and hobbies. Some are notes on existing mathematical tools to which I add personal insight. Note: this list contains only side projects and does not include any analysis of real cosmological data.
projects.title: Auto Safety Data Mining with NLP tools
projects.summary: Detect emerging vehicle safety defects from NHTSA consumer complaints using NLP prior to recall issuance. I built an end-to-end NLP pipeline using spaCy to mine unstructured text data from NHTSA consumer complaints. The pipeline performs topic detection, keyword extraction, and sentiment analysis to identify emerging safety issues in vehicles.
highlights: Unstructured data mining using NLP tools (spaCy) for topic detection, keyword extraction, and sentiment analysis.
Fetch data from multiple sources (NHTSA consumer complaints and official recalls) using the NHTSA REST API.
Carry out data cleaning and preprocessing in pandas, including handling missing values, de-duplication, text normalization, and feature engineering.
Sentence segmentation and tokenization using spaCy.
Named Entity Recognition (NER) to extract relevant entities for Auto Safety (e.g., vehicle models, defect types, etc.)
Co-occurrence analysis to identify relationships between entities and emerging safety issues.

projects.title: Machine Learning Specialization & Deep Learning
projects.summary: I have recently completed the Machine Learning Specialization by Andrew Ng of the Stanford AI Lab. With the rise of machine learning tools and applications, understanding the mathematical formalism behind these tools is crucial for any data-driven field, especially when selecting the right tool to solve a specific problem.
highlights: Supervised Learning Algorithms, Unsupervised Learning Algorithms, and Deep Learning Fundamentals.
Decision Trees, Random Forests, and Convolutional Neural Networks
Reinforcement Learning, Transfer Learning
Model Validation, Hyperparameter Tuning, Deep Learning Architectures
TensorFlow, Keras, PyTorch.

projects.title: Financial Modeling - Compound Interest Calculator
projects.summary: Outside of my research in Physics, I am passionate about understanding and modeling financial markets. Most recently, I built my own compound interest calculator from first principles. I noticed that many calculators online lack transparency in their calculations and can be limited for users who aren't financially trained. This custom-built calculator is intended for users with a financial background. Since this project's completion, I have been exploring the impact of home-building on the broader housing market using a Bayesian statistics approach. Stay tuned for more updates!
highlights: Custom and Interactive Compound Interest Calculator in Python.
All-in-one adjustable options for retirement forecasting.
Packaged in a pedagogical manner with full methodology breakdown
Built with Streamlit for easy web deployment - no backend.
Open-source code on GitHub — click the title to view.

projects.title: Model Parameter Estimation with Maximum Likelihood
projects.summary: I am passionate about statistics. Maximum likelihood estimation is a branch of statistics that is sometimes poorly motivated when taught in higher education. This project is an attempt at showing all the mathematical steps that lead to the maximum likelihood estimation method
highlights: Worked example of how maximum likelihood approach is used when estimating model parameters
Full formalism behind the worked example, explained step-by-step

projects.title: Simulation of Cosmic Microwave Background Signals
projects.summary: You might wonder what the signal from the first light of our Universe looks like when measured by the most sensitive detectors on the planet. In this project I explore exactly that! I simulate the universe at that time using existing cosmic microwave background (CMB) simulation packages in Python, and sample it with detectors
highlights: Simulate time-ordered data from a single realization of the temperature and polarization signal of our Universe 380,000 years after its birth
Realistic noise simulation
Using realistic curved sky projection

images.caption: Time-ordered data of CMB signal and noise

projects.title: Notes on Multivariate Linear Regression
projects.summary: One of the biggest challenges in an analytical field is converting a real-world problem into a mathematical one. This can be achieved with a mathematical parametric model. To extract information about the model one can use the process of linear regression. It is especially relevant in the field of statistics, machine learning, etc. The aim of this project is to illustrate the power of linear regression.
highlights: Linear regression for pedestrians
With some linear algebra, get a powerful estimating tool

projects.title: New York Times `Wordle` Game in Python
projects.summary: Ever played the popular NYT Wordle? Each day there's a secret five-letter word you need to guess in up to six tries. I coded the game in Python for fun; it needs some UI polish.
highlights: - Fun Python Game -

volunteer_experience.summary: Mentored and advised students on life skills outside of work, and facilitated vertical communication between students and faculty.
highlights: Secured funding for weekly meetings for 2 years in a row (2022-2024)
Led and facilitated these meetings with discussion topic focused on academic and personal development.
Organized social events to promote community building within the department.

degrees: Doctor of Philosophy in Physics - Experimental Cosmology

interests.name: Automotive Enthusiast
interests.summary: Starting from a young age, I was immersed in the thrill and excitement of automotive culture. I admire the complexity, effort, and design of most vehicles for the works of art that they are. Below are some photos of cars I have had the privilege to drive at least once.

interests.name: NCAA Division II Basketball Player
interests.summary: I played NCAA Division II basketball for Hillsdale College from 2015 to 2019. During my time there, I was part of a great team that made it to the NCAA Division II top 32 in 2018, and was part of the Academic Excellence team for 3 years in a row. Below are some action shots from my playing days.

publications.title: Calibration Measurements of the BICEP3 and BICEP Array CMB Polarimeters from 2017 to 2024
publications.author: Primary
publications.summary: This paper presents an overview of calibration measurements of the BICEP3 and BICEP Array polarimeters at the South Pole that enhances our understanding of instrument performance and systematic errors. It was presented as part of the International Society of Optics and Photonics (SPIE) Conference in June 2024 in Yokohama, Japan. The results are relevant for the ongoing efforts to measure excess B-mode polarization of the CMB.

publications.title: Pulsar scintillation through thick and thin: Bow shocks, bubbles, and the broader interstellar medium
publications.author: Secondary
publications.summary: Excellent paper on Pulsar scintillation by Stella Koch Ocker. Relevant to my undergraduate research using data I helped take during the summer of 2018.
